questions.question: An Ethernet LAN consists of 5 nodes (A, B, C, D, E). Node A sends data to node E. Plot the congestion window for this pair over a period of 10 seconds, assuming a bandwidth of 100 Mbps and a round-trip time of 2 ms. What is the maximum congestion window?
questions.hint: Assume random arrival of packets and exponential decay of congestion window

questions.question: A network engineer has 8 nodes in an Ethernet LAN. The nodes are connected to a switch, and two traffic nodes are established: Node A sending to Node C, and Node B sending to Node D. Plot the congestion window for both traffic nodes over a period of 20 seconds. What is the difference in congestion window between the two traffic nodes?
questions.hint: Assume a bandwidth of 200 Mbps and round-trip times of 3 ms for both pairs

questions.question: Suppose we have a 10-node Ethernet LAN, and we want to implement multiple traffic nodes. Node A sends data to Node F, Node B sends data to Node G, and Node C sends data to Node H. Assuming a bandwidth of 500 Mbps and round-trip times of 1 ms for all pairs, plot the congestion window for each traffic node over a period of 30 seconds. What is the average congestion window for all traffic nodes?
questions.hint: Assume random arrival of packets and exponential decay of congestion window
